rag_rules: AGREE assumptions and guarantees referencing component ports must be placed in component type scopes, not implementation scopes
When defining range constraints, use boolean helper declarations with explicit type alignment to avoid assumed/actual type mismatches
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks must declare helper variables with types that match their expressions
Assumptions should be placed in component type contracts when they refer to component ports
Guarantees should reference visible symbols that are properly declared in the component's scope
Use boolean helper declarations for range checks to ensure type consistency
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Helper declarations in AGREE must explicitly specify types that match their expressions
Assumptions and guarantees must reference valid visible symbols in the component scope
Range constraints should be expressed as boolean helper variables with clear type annotations
anti_patterns: Do not place component port references in implementation-level AGREE contracts
Avoid type mismatches between helper declaration types and expression types in AGREE
